findings.detail: Core concept intact: matrix root node branches to three row nodes, one row is labelled as a feature vector, and the terminal node captures 'rows=samples, cols=features'. All labels are readable, no overlaps, no truncation, no forward references.

The artifact_plan.diagram_intent called for 'a labelled rows-x-columns grid showing row numbers, column numbers, and individual cells; one row highlighted as a feature vector'. The delivered diagram is a tree hierarchy (matrix → rows → annotation) rather than a visual grid. Column numbers and individual cells are absent. The pedagogical message (rows = data samples, rows=samples / cols=features) is still conveyed correctly, so this is a soft finding only — the diagram passes. On a future revision, consider a grid-style layout (e.g., a subgraph with column labels and one shaded row) to match the stated intent more closely.
